primary_diagnosis.name: Drug-induced linear IgA bullous dermatosis (mild localised); severe cutaneous adverse reaction not fully excludable remotely

pathophysiology_summary: Linear IgA bullous dermatosis is a subepidermal autoimmune blistering disease (linear IgA deposition at the basement membrane), frequently drug-induced (classically vancomycin, also other antibiotics), producing tense clear vesicles often in annular 'string-of-pearls' clusters. It is usually localised and resolves on stopping the culprit drug. Because the eruption follows a new antibiotic, the critical task is to exclude a severe cutaneous adverse reaction — Stevens-Johnson syndrome/TEN (mucosal involvement, skin sloughing, positive Nikolsky, systemic prodrome) and anaphylaxis (angioedema, wheeze) — none present here (isolated forearm tense vesicles, clear mucosa, negative sloughing, well patient). The immediate actions are to STOP the antibiotic and arrange urgent in-person dermatology review, because drug-induced blistering can evolve.

differential.evidence_basis: Tense clear vesicles in annular clusters after a new antibiotic.
A blistering eruption after a new drug mandates excluding SJS/TEN — screen mucosa, sloughing, systemic prodrome.
No honey-coloured crusting or purulent fluid; tense clear vesicles.
No mucosal involvement/sloughing/prodrome (DI-001), no anaphylaxis (DI-002), tense annular vesicles post-antibiotic (DI-003) — a localised drug-induced bullous dermatosis.
Currently excluded on history, but drug-induced blistering can evolve — retained as the escalation trigger with urgent in-person review.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: STOP the antibiotic, exclude SJS/anaphylaxis, potent topical steroid, urgent in-person dermatology/GP review (biopsy/immunofluorescence, alternative antibiotic), and strong SJS/anaphylaxis safety-nets. Neither ED over-triage of a well localised eruption nor failure to stop the drug/safety-net is acceptable.

red_flags.description: SJS/TEN features — mucosal (mouth/eye/genital) involvement, skin sloughing in sheets, positive Nikolsky, or systemic prodrome
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Untreated SJS/TEN causes extensive epidermal loss and death.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Reassuring a drug-induced blistering eruption without a mucosal/sloughing screen or a strong SJS safety-net, missing early SJS/TEN.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Failing to stop the culprit drug
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Not advising immediate cessation of the antibiotic, the single most important action.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Stop the antibiotic; potent topical steroid; urgent in-person dermatology/GP review; strong SJS/anaphylaxis safety-nets; ED for those red flags.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reassuring a drug-induced blistering eruption remotely could miss evolving SJS/TEN; over-triaging a well localised eruption wastes emergency resources.
